evidence.summary: Ealing Council's Southall regeneration page, covering the Southall Waterside development on the former gasworks site and wider plans for the Southall area.

title: Ely North Sustainable Urban Extension
description: East Cambridgeshire District Council's adopted Local Plan allocates land to the north of Ely for a major sustainable urban extension of approximately 3,000 new homes, employment land, schools, community facilities, and supporting transport infrastructure. The development is designed to meet housing need in this high-growth sub-region while maintaining the setting of the cathedral city.
statusRationale: Allocation confirmed in adopted Local Plan; planning applications and infrastructure agreements progressing through the local planning system.
locationDescription: North of Ely, East Cambridgeshire

evidence.title: Local Plan – East Cambridgeshire District Council
evidence.summary: East Cambridgeshire District Council Local Plan page, setting out strategic allocations including the Ely North sustainable urban extension and associated infrastructure delivery requirements.

title: Cranbrook New Town Expansion
description: East Devon District Council continues to oversee the planned expansion of Cranbrook, Devon's newest town and one of England's few post-war new settlements, with over 3,500 homes delivered to date. The adopted Local Plan provides for continued phased growth with new community infrastructure, retail, and employment land. The town is served by Cranbrook railway station with direct connections to Exeter.
statusRationale: Town growing on schedule; housing delivery continuous; new community facilities and town centre progressing.
locationDescription: Cranbrook, East Devon

evidence.title: Cranbrook – East Devon District Council
evidence.summary: East Devon District Council planning policy page for Cranbrook, covering the town's expansion phases, infrastructure delivery, and Local Plan allocations for further growth.

title: Whitehill and Bordon Eco-Town Regeneration
description: East Hampshire District Council is leading the transformation of Whitehill and Bordon, a former army garrison town, into a thriving eco-town with 3,350 new homes, employment land, new schools, a sports hub, extensive green infrastructure, and improved transport links. The project is one of the UK's largest garrison-town regeneration programmes, with delivery ongoing since the military vacated the site in 2015.
statusRationale: Substantial housing delivery achieved; major community infrastructure including schools and Louisburg Barracks leisure hub delivered; later residential phases progressing to plan.
locationDescription: Whitehill and Bordon, East Hampshire

evidence.title: Whitehill and Bordon – Project Website
evidence.summary: Official project website for the Whitehill and Bordon eco-town regeneration programme, covering delivery progress on new homes, schools, community facilities, employment land, and green infrastructure.

title: Harlow and Gilston Garden Town
description: Harlow and Gilston Garden Town is a nationally significant development of up to 23,000 new homes straddling East Hertfordshire and Harlow, centred on the Gilston Area to the north of the existing Harlow New Town. East Hertfordshire District Council co-leads delivery alongside Harlow Council and Hertfordshire County Council, with the scheme including major transport infrastructure, new schools, community facilities, and extensive green corridors.
statusRationale: Garden Town status granted; master planning and infrastructure agreements progressing; first Gilston housing completions expected in the mid-2020s.
locationDescription: Gilston Area, East Hertfordshire and Harlow
locationSource: Harlow and Gilston Garden City Partnership

evidence.title: Harlow and Gilston Garden City – Official Site
evidence.source: Harlow and Gilston Garden City Partnership
evidence.summary: Official website for the Harlow and Gilston Garden Town, covering the vision, masterplan, phased development programme, infrastructure delivery, and partnership governance.

title: Skegness and Mablethorpe Long-Term Plan for Towns
description: Both Skegness and Mablethorpe in East Lindsey were included in the UK Government's Long-Term Plan for Towns programme (announced October 2023), each receiving a £20 million endowment to fund a 10-year regeneration programme. East Lindsey District Council is working with local town boards to develop investment plans covering high streets, housing, heritage, transport, leisure, and community facilities.
statusRationale: Funding confirmed and town boards established October 2023; investment plans in development and early spend as of 2024–25.
locationDescription: Skegness and Mablethorpe, East Lindsey, Lincolnshire
locationSource: UK Government / East Lindsey District Council

evidence.title: Long-Term Plan for Towns: The 100 Towns – GOV.UK
evidence.summary: UK Government publication announcing the 100 towns selected for the Long-Term Plan for Towns programme, including Skegness and Mablethorpe in East Lindsey, each receiving £20m over 10 years.

title: Bridlington Town Deal
description: East Riding of Yorkshire Council secured a £25.1 million Town Deal for Bridlington in 2021, funding regeneration of the Yorkshire seaside town's seafront, heritage, skills, and connectivity. Key projects include Bridlington Spa refurbishment, public realm improvements on the South Promenade, a learning and skills hub, and cycling infrastructure. The programme aims to boost the visitor economy and improve year-round services for residents.
statusRationale: Multiple project strands in delivery; Bridlington Spa works progressing; overall programme broadly on track.
locationDescription: Bridlington, East Riding of Yorkshire

evidence.title: Bridlington Town Deal – East Riding of Yorkshire Council
evidence.summary: East Riding of Yorkshire Council page on the Bridlington Town Deal covering the £25.1m investment programme, project pipeline including Bridlington Spa, and town board governance.

title: Burton upon Trent Town Deal
description: East Staffordshire Borough Council signed one of the largest Town Deals in England, receiving £37.5 million from the UK Government's Towns Fund for Burton upon Trent. The investment funds The Maltings regeneration (a new civic and skills hub), the Brewers Quarter mixed-use development, active travel and cycling improvements, and digital connectivity upgrades across the town.
statusRationale: Several projects completed or in final delivery; Town Deal programme broadly on track with major milestones met as of 2024–25.
locationDescription: Burton upon Trent, East Staffordshire

evidence.title: Towns Fund – East Staffordshire Borough Council
evidence.summary: East Staffordshire Borough Council's Towns Fund page covering the £37.5m Town Deal for Burton upon Trent, including The Maltings, Brewers Quarter, and active travel schemes.

title: Lowestoft Town Deal
description: East Suffolk Council secured £25 million through the UK Government's Towns Fund for Lowestoft, the UK's most easterly town. Key projects include the Tides Learning and Innovation Centre — a purpose-built higher and further education facility that opened in 2023 — alongside flood-risk management works, seafront regeneration, and arts and culture investment. The Town Deal forms part of a wider programme to diversify Lowestoft's economy beyond its traditional fishing and offshore-energy industries.
statusRationale: Tides Learning and Innovation Centre opened 2023; other workstreams in delivery; programme broadly on schedule.
locationDescription: Lowestoft, East Suffolk

evidence.title: Lowestoft Town Deal – East Suffolk Council
evidence.summary: East Suffolk Council's page on the Lowestoft Town Deal covering the £25m investment programme including the Tides Learning and Innovation Centre, flood-risk management, and seafront improvements.

title: Devonshire Park Restoration and Congress Theatre Redevelopment
description: Eastbourne Borough Council led a multi-phase Heritage Lottery Fund and council-funded restoration of Devonshire Park, centred on the Congress Theatre and Winter Garden. The Congress Theatre's £42 million restoration involved full internal refurbishment, accessible upgrades, and restoration of its Art Deco exterior. The theatre reopened in 2024 as a flagship cultural venue for the South East coast, anchoring the wider regeneration of Eastbourne's Devonshire Quarter.
statusRationale: Congress Theatre restoration completed and reopened 2024; Devonshire Park quarter fully operational.
locationDescription: Devonshire Park, Eastbourne, East Sussex

evidence.title: Devonshire Park – Official Venue Website
evidence.summary: Official website for Devonshire Park, Eastbourne, covering the restored Congress Theatre, Winter Garden, and the Devonshire Quarter cultural regeneration project led by Eastbourne Borough Council.

title: Eastleigh Town Centre Regeneration Masterplan
description: Eastleigh Borough Council has adopted a Town Centre Masterplan to guide the regeneration of Eastleigh town centre, improving the public realm around the railway station and high street, supporting mixed-use redevelopment, and enhancing pedestrian and cycle connectivity. The programme is supported by UK Shared Prosperity Fund investment and planning policy interventions to attract private development.
statusRationale: Masterplan adopted and UKSPF projects under way; major development sites in pre-application and planning stages as of 2024–25.
locationDescription: Eastleigh Town Centre, Hampshire

evidence.title: Planning Policy – Eastleigh Borough Council
evidence.summary: Eastleigh Borough Council planning policy page covering the Town Centre Masterplan, site allocations, and regeneration strategies for Eastleigh town centre.

title: Elmbridge UK Shared Prosperity Fund Investment Plan
description: Elmbridge Borough Council is delivering a programme of investments under the UK Shared Prosperity Fund (2022–25), supporting local businesses, community facilities, public realm improvements, and active-travel routes across the borough's towns including Walton-on-Thames, Esher, and Cobham.
statusRationale: UKSPF programme operational 2022–25; projects being delivered across the borough on schedule.
locationDescription: Walton-on-Thames and Elmbridge Borough, Surrey

evidence.title: UK Shared Prosperity Fund – Elmbridge Borough Council
evidence.summary: Elmbridge Borough Council page on the UK Shared Prosperity Fund investment plan, covering business support, community facilities, and public realm projects across the borough.

title: Meridian Water Regeneration, Enfield
description: Meridian Water is one of London's largest regeneration schemes, led by Enfield Council on an 85-hectare brownfield site along the River Lee in north-east Enfield. The 20-year programme will deliver 10,000 new homes and 6,000 jobs across a series of phased mixed-use neighbourhoods. Phase 1 housing is occupied; Phase 2 planning consents have been secured; the council has committed over £600m in public investment to unlock the site and provide infrastructure.
statusRationale: Phase 1 residential completed and occupied; Phase 2 construction and infrastructure works under way; long-term programme with complex ongoing land assembly.
locationDescription: Meridian Water, Lee Valley, Enfield, London

evidence.title: Meridian Water – Official Project Website
evidence.summary: Official website for the Meridian Water regeneration programme, led by Enfield Council, covering the 10,000-home, 6,000-job scheme, phased development plans, and community engagement.

evidence.title: Meridian Water – Enfield Council
evidence.summary: Enfield Council's Meridian Water information page providing planning background, council investment commitments, and links to planning applications and project-progress updates.

title: Epping Forest Local Plan: North Weald Airfield Strategic Allocation
description: Epping Forest District Council adopted its Local Plan in 2023 after an extended examination, allocating the former North Weald Airfield site for approximately 1,630 new homes alongside employment, community facilities, and green infrastructure. The airfield site is the district's largest single development allocation and is a central element of meeting the borough's housing need within a predominantly Green Belt authority.
statusRationale: Local Plan adopted 2023; site in early planning-application stages; delivery expected over the next decade.
locationDescription: North Weald Airfield, Epping Forest District, Essex

evidence.title: Adopted Local Plan – Epping Forest District Council
evidence.summary: Epping Forest District Council adopted Local Plan page, setting out strategic allocations including the North Weald Airfield site and supporting infrastructure and delivery frameworks.

title: Epsom Town Centre Vision
description: Epsom and Ewell Borough Council has published a Town Centre Vision to guide the long-term regeneration of Epsom town centre, focusing on the area around the Ashley Centre, the Clock Tower, and the station gateway. The vision identifies opportunities for mixed-use redevelopment, public realm enhancement, improved pedestrian and cycling links, and a diversified retail and leisure offer to sustain the town centre's viability.
statusRationale: Town Centre Vision published; individual sites in pre-application discussions and early planning stages as of 2024–25.
locationDescription: Epsom Town Centre, Surrey

evidence.title: Planning Policy – Epsom and Ewell Borough Council
evidence.summary: Epsom and Ewell Borough Council planning policy page covering the Epsom Town Centre Vision, site allocations, and regeneration strategies for the town centre area.

title: Long Eaton Town Deal – Galaxy Row Redevelopment
description: The £25 million Long Eaton Town Deal is transforming the derelict Galaxy cinema site on Derby Road into a mixed-use scheme delivering 16 flats, 4 townhouses and 3 commercial units. Demolition of the Galaxy Row buildings was completed in March 2025, with construction by partners Mypad and Tuntum Housing expected over approximately 4 months.
statusRationale: Demolition milestone reached March 2025; construction underway with confirmed partners and funding in place.
locationDescription: Galaxy Row / Derby Road, Long Eaton, Erewash

evidence.title: Rebirth of Galaxy Row: demolition marks major Town Deal milestone
evidence.summary: Demolition of the derelict Galaxy cinema on Derby Road, Long Eaton, completed as part of the £25m Long Eaton Town Deal. The site will deliver 16 flats, 4 townhouses and 3 commercial units.

title: Exeter Pride in Place – Whipton and Heavitree Neighbourhood Investment
description: Exeter City Council has been awarded £20 million in Pride in Place funding from central government to invest in the Whipton and Heavitree neighbourhoods over 10 years. A new Neighbourhood Board will be established with residents, councillors and local organisations to co-create a Pride in Place Plan determining how the funding is spent on improvements to local communities and places.
statusRationale: Funding formally awarded March 2026; Neighbourhood Board being established to begin delivery planning.
locationDescription: Whipton and Heavitree neighbourhoods, Exeter

evidence.title: Exeter awarded £20m Pride in Place funding for neighbourhoods
evidence.summary: Exeter awarded £20 million Pride in Place funding to be spent over 10 years in Whipton and Heavitree. A Neighbourhood Board will co-create an investment plan with local residents.

title: Fareham Town Centre Regeneration Strategy
description: Fareham Borough Council published a 10-year Town Centre Regeneration Strategy in February 2025, setting out a bold vision for the future of Fareham town centre. Shaped by community engagement, the strategy is underpinned by four pillars – Pride, Purpose, Perception and Personality – and aims to create more homes, new public spaces, and a diverse retail, leisure and entertainment offer. A public masterplan consultation ran to April 2026.
statusRationale: Strategy published and masterplan consultation underway; delivery and funding for individual projects not yet confirmed.
locationDescription: Fareham Town Centre, Hampshire

evidence.title: Fareham Town Centre Regeneration
evidence.summary: 10-year Fareham Town Centre Regeneration Strategy published February 2025. Four key pillars; seeks new homes, improved public spaces, diverse retail and leisure, shaped by community engagement. Masterplan consultation open to April 2026.

title: March Future High Streets Fund Town Centre Transformation
description: Fenland District Council secured funding through the Government's Future High Streets Fund to transform March town centre. Projects include public realm improvements, refurbishment of the historic town hall, new market infrastructure and cycling and walking enhancements. A contractor was appointed and key projects are being delivered as part of the March Masterplan.
statusRationale: Contractor appointed and projects in delivery; multiple phases of work underway or complete.
locationDescription: March Town Centre, Fenland, Cambridgeshire

evidence.title: March Future High Streets Fund Projects
evidence.summary: Overview of Future High Streets Fund projects being delivered in March town centre, including public realm improvements, heritage building refurbishment and active travel infrastructure.

title: Otterpool Park Garden Town
description: Otterpool Park is a new garden town being developed on approximately 770 hectares in the Folkestone and Hythe district. The council-owned development aims to deliver up to 10,000 homes alongside schools, community facilities, employment land and extensive green space. It is one of the Government's designated garden communities and is being brought forward by Otterpool Park LLP, a joint venture between Folkestone & Hythe District Council and Homes England.
statusRationale: Outline planning permission secured; detailed planning and infrastructure works in early stages of delivery.
locationDescription: Otterpool Park, near Sellindge, Folkestone and Hythe, Kent

evidence.title: Otterpool Park
evidence.summary: Council project page for Otterpool Park garden town: up to 10,000 new homes on 770 hectares, joint venture between the council and Homes England, one of the Government's designated garden communities.

title: Cinderford Northern Quarter Regeneration
description: The Cinderford Northern Quarter is a major regeneration scheme transforming a formerly derelict industrial site in Cinderford town centre into a new mixed-use quarter. The Forest of Dean District Council, working with Gloucestershire College and other partners, is delivering new educational facilities, housing, business space and public realm across a substantial brownfield site.
statusRationale: Active regeneration programme with multiple phases; Gloucestershire College campus delivered and further phases ongoing.
locationDescription: Cinderford Northern Quarter, Cinderford, Forest of Dean

evidence.title: Cinderford Northern Quarter
evidence.summary: Council project page for the Cinderford Northern Quarter regeneration scheme – transforming derelict industrial land in Cinderford town centre with education, housing, employment and public realm.

title: Kirkham Futures Programme
description: The Kirkham Futures Programme is a multi-million pound regeneration of Kirkham town centre funded through the Government's £6.29 million Future High Street Fund and £3.27 million High Streets Heritage Action Zone award. The programme has delivered new public realm in Market Square and Poulton Street, refurbishment of 25+ shopfronts, heritage building restoration and installation of heritage lighting. In November 2024, Fylde Council approved the final £289,678 capital allocation to complete the shopfront improvement phase.
statusRationale: Final phase funded and in delivery; programme substantially complete with public realm and shopfronts delivered.
locationDescription: Market Square and Poulton Street, Kirkham, Fylde, Lancashire

evidence.title: Fylde Council Approves Final Capital Expenditure for Kirkham Futures Programme
evidence.summary: Final £289,678 capital expenditure approved for Kirkham Futures shopfront improvements. Total programme funded by £6.29m Future High Streets Fund and £3.27m Heritage Action Zone. Over 25 shopfronts and properties revitalised.

title: Greater Carlton £20 Million Regeneration Plan
description: Gedling Borough Council and the Greater Carlton Neighbourhood Board unveiled a landmark 10-year, £20 million regeneration plan for Greater Carlton in November 2025. Shaped by hundreds of residents through surveys, workshops and community engagement, the plan has five missions: raising skill levels, improving youth provision, tackling health inequalities, improving the environment, and strengthening community cohesion. Key areas receiving targeted support include Netherfield, Carlton Hill and Cavendish. Delivery is set to begin April 2026.
statusRationale: Plan formally adopted; delivery confirmed to begin April 2026 with East Midlands Combined County Authority as key partner.
locationDescription: Greater Carlton (Netherfield, Carlton Hill, Cavendish), Gedling, Nottinghamshire

evidence.title: Greater Carlton: A 10-Year, £20 Million Transformation Plan Unveiled
evidence.summary: Gedling Borough Council and Greater Carlton Neighbourhood Board unveil £20m 10-year regeneration plan with five missions covering skills, youth, health, environment and community. Delivery begins April 2026.

title: The Forum – Gloucester City Centre Regeneration
description: The Forum is a landmark mixed-use regeneration development in Gloucester city centre, transforming the former Kings Square area. Developed by Gloucester City Council, it includes a new university campus, library, offices, hotel, residential units and public realm. The development won the Regeneration Project of the Year at the Michelmores Property Awards 2025. A luxury Hotel Indigo opened in 2026 as part of the scheme.
statusRationale: Development substantially complete; Hotel Indigo opened 2026; won national Regeneration Project of the Year 2025.
locationDescription: Kings Square / Forum site, Gloucester city centre

evidence.title: Gloucester's Forum wins Regeneration Project of the Year
evidence.summary: The Forum crowned Regeneration Project of the Year at Michelmores Property Awards 2025. Judges described it as 'frankly brilliant' – mixed-use city centre development with university, library, hotel and public realm.

title: Regenerating Gosport's Waterfront
description: Gosport Borough Council is leading a once-in-a-generation opportunity to revitalise and maximise the potential of Gosport's historic waterfront. The programme aims to create a vibrant waterfront destination with new homes, commercial uses, improved public realm and better connectivity between the waterfront and town centre, capitalising on the town's unique harbour setting opposite Portsmouth.
statusRationale: Waterfront vision and strategy established; detailed project delivery timelines and funding not yet fully confirmed.
locationDescription: Gosport Waterfront, Hampshire

evidence.title: Regenerating Gosport's Waterfront
evidence.summary: Council waterfront regeneration programme page. Described as a 'once-in-a-generation opportunity' to revitalise Gosport's waterfront with new homes, commercial uses and improved public realm.

title: Gravesend Transport Quarter
description: Gravesham Borough Council, working with Kent County Council and the South-East Local Enterprise Partnership, delivered the Gravesend Transport Quarter in three phases, improving rail, bus, cycling and pedestrian links in the town centre. Phase 1 created a Community Square and upgraded Gravesend Railway Station (new platforms, accessible bridge, cycle hub). Phase 2 built the new Rathmore Road link and station forecourt. Phase 3 added four new bus stops, Fastrack BRT stops and electric bus charging on Garrick Street.
statusRationale: All three phases delivered and substantially complete; funded by SELEP Local Growth Fund and Strategic Transport Infrastructure Programme.
locationDescription: Gravesend Town Centre and Railway Station, Gravesham, Kent

evidence.title: Transport Quarter – Major Projects and Regeneration
evidence.summary: Three-phase Transport Quarter delivering rail, bus, cycling and pedestrian improvements in Gravesend town centre, funded by SELEP Local Growth Fund. Includes upgraded railway station, new Rathmore Road link, bus interchange and Fastrack BRT stops.

title: Charlton Riverside Regeneration
description: The Royal Borough of Greenwich is leading the comprehensive regeneration of Charlton Riverside, a major brownfield Thames-side site. The programme aims to deliver thousands of new homes including affordable housing, employment space, improved riverside open space and new community infrastructure, transforming a former industrial area into a sustainable new neighbourhood.
statusRationale: Masterplan and supplementary planning document in place; individual development sites at varying stages of planning and delivery.
locationDescription: Charlton Riverside, Royal Borough of Greenwich, London

evidence.title: Charlton Riverside
evidence.summary: Council's Charlton Riverside regeneration programme page. Major Thames-side brownfield regeneration delivering new homes, employment and riverside improvements on a former industrial site.

title: North Street Regeneration, Guildford
description: St Edward (Berkeley Homes Group) is developing the North Street Regeneration scheme on approximately 4 acres in Guildford town centre. The project will deliver 477 highly sustainable new homes (including 47 affordable), 2.2 acres of public realm with a wellness garden and new town square, a refurbished bus station, pedestrianisation of North Street, 115 new trees and new medical centre premises. Demolition was completed by early 2026 and construction works are underway.
statusRationale: Demolition complete; utility diversions and road changes underway as at early 2026; construction progressing.
locationDescription: North Street / Commercial Road / Leapale Road, Guildford town centre, Surrey

evidence.title: Find out about North Street Regeneration
evidence.summary: 477 new homes, 2.2 acres of public realm, refurbished bus station, pedestrianised North Street, 115 new trees and medical centre. Developer St Edward (Berkeley Group). Demolition complete; construction commenced early 2026.

title: Woodberry Down Estate Regeneration
description: The Woodberry Down regeneration is one of Europe's largest estate regeneration programmes. Hackney Council, working with Berkeley Group and Notting Hill Genesis, is transforming a 1950s estate near Finsbury Park into a new neighbourhood delivering approximately 5,500 new homes alongside schools, community facilities and improved parkland around the Woodberry Wetlands. The regeneration is being delivered in phases over approximately 25 years.
statusRationale: Multiple phases complete; delivery continuing with strong partnership and confirmed phasing programme.
locationDescription: Woodberry Down Estate, Manor House / Finsbury Park, Hackney, London

evidence.title: Woodberry Down regeneration
evidence.summary: One of Europe's largest estate regeneration programmes: approximately 5,500 new homes replacing a 1950s estate near Finsbury Park, delivered in phases by Hackney Council with Berkeley Group and Notting Hill Genesis.

title: Runcorn Station Quarter – Mersey Gateway Regeneration
description: Runcorn Station Quarter is one of 14 areas within Halton Borough Council's £1 billion Mersey Gateway Regeneration Plus Plan. The project removes the 'trumpet loop' road around the Silver Jubilee Bridge, replaces it with a new five-arm roundabout improving connections between the bridge, Runcorn Town Centre and the railway station. A new public piazza is being constructed outside the station. The Silver Jubilee Bridge has been reopened to cyclists, pedestrians and vehicles following significant maintenance.
statusRationale: New roundabout and station forecourt constructed; Silver Jubilee Bridge reopened; further development phases progressing as part of the £1bn Mersey Gateway Regeneration Plus Plan.
locationDescription: Runcorn Station / Silver Jubilee Bridge area, Halton, Cheshire

evidence.title: Runcorn Station Quarter
evidence.summary: Part of the £1bn Mersey Gateway Regeneration Plus Plan. Trumpet loop removed, new five-arm roundabout built, Silver Jubilee Bridge reopened to all users, new station piazza under construction.

title: White City Regeneration Area
description: The White City Regeneration Area in Hammersmith & Fulham covers 110 hectares including Shepherds Bush town centre, the BBC Television Centre and Queen's Park Rangers Football Club. The council is focused on attracting new homes and jobs east of Wood Lane and north of Westfield London, with a particular emphasis on creative industries and bio-medical innovation. BBC Television Centre has been transformed. An Affordable Workspace Strategy 2026-2031 with over £8 million of developer-secured funding supports STEM and creative businesses in the district.
statusRationale: Active regeneration programme with BBC Television Centre transformation complete; continued investment in innovation and residential development.
locationDescription: White City / Wood Lane / Shepherds Bush, Hammersmith and Fulham, London

evidence.title: White City Regeneration Area
evidence.summary: 110-hectare regeneration area including Shepherds Bush, BBC Television Centre and QPR. Focus on new homes, creative industries and bio-medical innovation east of Wood Lane.

evidence.title: Investing in new affordable workspaces so local businesses can succeed
evidence.summary: Over £8m secured from developers for affordable workspaces in the White City Innovation District and other regeneration areas, as part of the Upstream London industrial strategy.

title: Lutterworth and Market Harborough Town Centre Masterplans
description: Harborough District Council secured £2.17 million from the UK Shared Prosperity Fund to deliver investment across the district. A £1 million allocation is being used to deliver the Lutterworth and Market Harborough town centre masterplans, including improvements to cycling and walking infrastructure and enhancements to four larger village centres: Broughton Astley, Kibworth, Fleckney and Great Glen. Works in Broughton Astley were completed in 2023 with further phases ongoing.
statusRationale: UKSPF investment plan formally approved; works delivered in Broughton Astley; further village improvements continuing to 2025.
locationDescription: Market Harborough and Lutterworth town centres; Broughton Astley, Kibworth, Fleckney, Great Glen – Harborough District, Leicestershire

evidence.title: Harborough district to benefit from Government funding
evidence.summary: £2.17m UK Shared Prosperity Fund award confirmed. £1m allocated to Lutterworth and Market Harborough town centre masterplans including cycling, walking and public realm improvements.

title: Tottenham Regeneration Programme
description: Haringey Council is leading a major long-term regeneration programme across Tottenham, one of the largest regeneration areas in London. The programme covers multiple neighbourhoods including Tottenham Hale, High Road West, Love Lane and Bruce Grove, delivering thousands of new homes, improved public spaces, new employment space and enhanced transport connectivity. The High Road West scheme will deliver over 2,500 new homes around Tottenham Hotspur Stadium.
statusRationale: Multiple regeneration sites in active planning and delivery across the Tottenham area.
locationDescription: Tottenham, Haringey, London (multiple sites including Tottenham Hale, High Road West)

evidence.title: Tottenham
evidence.summary: Council's Tottenham regeneration hub page covering Tottenham Hale, High Road West (2,500+ homes near Spurs stadium), Love Lane and Bruce Grove neighbourhood regeneration areas.

title: Harlow Innovation Park – Final Phase
description: Harlow Council secured an agreement for the final phase of Harlow Innovation Park, a major employment-led development on the former Templefields industrial area. The innovation park is part of the wider Harlow & Gilston Garden Town programme and aims to create a cluster of high-value science, technology and innovation businesses, delivering new jobs and investment in the town.
statusRationale: Final phase agreement announced; earlier phases delivered with established occupiers in place.
locationDescription: Harlow Innovation Park, Templefields, Harlow, Essex

evidence.title: Agreement announced for final phase of Harlow Innovation Park
evidence.summary: Agreement secured for the final development phase of Harlow Innovation Park, delivering high-value science and technology employment space as part of the Harlow & Gilston Garden Town programme.

title: Grange Farm Estate Regeneration, South Harrow
description: Harrow Council is transforming the 1960s Grange Farm Estate in South Harrow through a major long-term regeneration programme. Phase 2A demolition was completed in March 2026, clearing the way for 45 new affordable homes (including up to 36 council homes) across two blocks with wheelchair-accessible units and improved landscaping. The overall vision is to deliver approximately 500 new homes, a community centre and high-quality public spaces. 89 new homes were delivered in earlier phases. The programme has been closely co-designed with residents since 2015.
statusRationale: Phase 2A demolition complete March 2026; 45 new affordable homes now progressing to construction; 89 homes already delivered in earlier phases.
locationDescription: Grange Farm Estate, South Harrow, London Borough of Harrow

evidence.title: Next chapter for Grange Farm underway
evidence.summary: Phase 2A demolition complete. 45 new affordable homes including 36 council homes to be built in two blocks. Total vision: ~500 homes, community centre and high-quality public spaces. Council flagship action.

title: Hart Local Cycling and Walking Infrastructure Plan (LCWIP)
description: Hart District Council, in partnership with Hampshire County Council, produced and adopted a Local Cycling and Walking Infrastructure Plan (LCWIP) in March 2024. The plan sets out a network of proposed cycle and walking route improvements across the district and will be used to secure developer contributions toward cycling and pedestrian infrastructure as part of new planning permissions.
statusRationale: Plan adopted by Hart District Council in March 2024 and by Hampshire County Council in June 2024; now in active use as a material planning consideration.
locationDescription: Hart District, Hampshire (centred on Fleet town centre)

evidence.title: Local Cycling and Walking Infrastructure Plan – Hart District Council
evidence.summary: Hart District Council page confirming the LCWIP was produced with Hampshire County Council and adopted in March 2024. The plan indicates improvements needed to walking and cycling infrastructure and will help secure improvements through developer contributions.

title: Hartlepool Waterfront Programme and Pride in Place
description: Hartlepool Borough Council is delivering a major programme of waterfront and town-centre regeneration funded by the Town Deal (£25 million, 2021) and the Pride in Place programme (£20 million, 2024 onwards). The Waterfront Programme focuses on investing in cultural, heritage and leisure infrastructure at the marina to create a landmark destination. The Screen Industries Production Village on Church Street forms a further strand, converting existing buildings into production facilities for film and TV. Pride in Place (formerly Long-Term Plan for Towns) funds continue the programme beyond the Town Deal, supporting public realm and economic recovery.
statusRationale: Town Deal projects are in delivery; Pride in Place funding of £20 million confirmed, adding to the previously awarded Town Deal and Levelling Up Fund money.
locationDescription: Hartlepool town centre and marina waterfront, County Durham

evidence.title: Regeneration – Hartlepool Borough Council
evidence.summary: Official council regeneration page describing the Waterfront Programme, Town Deal (£25m), Pride in Place (£20m) and Screen Industries Production Village. The page sets out the full investment programme for Hartlepool town centre and marina.

title: Hastings Foreshore Trust – Pelham Beach Playground Renewal
description: The Foreshore Trust, managed by Hastings Borough Council, delivered a significant upgrade to Pelham Beach Playground on the seafront. The project replaced and upgraded play equipment, installed new surfaces and interactive play boards, and added seating areas for families. Works were carried out by Eibe Play. The playground was formally reopened on 24 July 2025 following the works.
statusRationale: Works completed and playground officially reopened on 24 July 2025 with a public launch event attended by senior councillors and local MP.
locationDescription: Pelham Beach, Hastings seafront, East Sussex

evidence.title: Foreshore Trust 2024 Pelham Playground Renewal Scheme – Hastings Borough Council
evidence.summary: Council regeneration project page reporting that Pelham Beach Playground was successfully reopened on 24 July 2025 after a major upgrade by contractor Eibe Play, including new play equipment, surfaces, interactive boards and seating.

evidence.title: Pride in Place – Hastings Borough Council
evidence.summary: Hastings is one of the towns receiving Pride in Place (Plan for Neighbourhoods) funding from the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government. The programme was renamed from Long-Term Plan for Towns on 25 September 2025 and supports wider regeneration across the borough.

title: Havant Town Centre – Bulbeck Road Residential Regeneration
description: Havant Borough Council secured £1.65 million from the Department for Levelling Up and Communities' Brownfield Land Release Fund 2 (BLRF2) in November 2022 to demolish the underused Bulbeck Road car park and prepare the site for up to 91 new town-centre homes. This is Phase 1 of a larger residential-led regeneration programme for Havant town centre that will also integrate the railway and bus stations with the Civic Centre Campus. The Council is also working with the High Streets Task Force, which selected Havant in 2022.
statusRationale: BLRF2 grant funding secured and site preparation progressing; the final housing form is yet to be determined and full delivery is subject to further development decisions.
locationDescription: Bulbeck Road car park, Havant town centre, Hampshire

evidence.title: Havant Town Centre Regeneration – Havant Borough Council
evidence.summary: Council page confirming £1.65m from Brownfield Land Release Fund 2 to demolish Bulbeck Road car park and prepare for 91 new town-centre homes. Describes the wider vision for residential-led Havant town centre regeneration including better station integration.

title: Rainham and Beam Park Housing Zone and New Station
description: Havering Council's Rainham and Beam Park Housing Zone aims to transform a declining industrial area into a vibrant new urban community of up to 3,500 new homes over 10–15 years, supported by new transport, schools, health and green infrastructure. The zone's centrepiece is a proposed new Beam Park railway station on the c2c line, which will act as a civic hub and catalyst for over 5,000 homes and is pivotal to the wider London Riverside regeneration. In March 2026 the Government and Mayor of London announced support via the New Homes Accelerator to address the station funding gap caused by cost inflation.
statusRationale: Housing Zone is active and homes in delivery, but the critical Beam Park station project faces a funding gap due to inflation and cost increases, with the Council and GLA seeking solutions as of March 2026.
locationDescription: Rainham and Beam Park, south-east London Borough of Havering, near Rainham town centre

evidence.title: Rainham and Beam Park – London Borough of Havering
evidence.summary: Official council page describing the Housing Zone vision for 3,500 homes, the importance of Beam Park station to unlock 5,000+ homes and over £1bn investment, and wider social infrastructure including schools, health centre and 60,000 sq ft commercial space.

evidence.title: Leader's Statement: Beam Park Station update – Havering Council
evidence.summary: Council leader statement (25 March 2026) confirming Government and GLA support through the New Homes Accelerator for Beam Park station, while acknowledging cost increases due to inflation and that partners are working to find solutions to the funding gap.

title: Leominster High Street Heritage Action Zone
description: Herefordshire Council, Historic England, Leominster Town Council and other partners collectively invested approximately £1.65 million in the Leominster High Street Heritage Action Zone. The project delivered improvements to pavements, roads and public spaces in the market town centre, restoring natural stone paving, widening footways, improving junctions for pedestrians and renovating historic shopfronts and buildings to reflect original character. Works completed by December 2024.
statusRationale: Works completed by December 2024. Local councillors and visitors confirmed the quality of finished works and the positive impact on the town centre.
locationDescription: Leominster town centre, Herefordshire

evidence.title: Restoring Leominster's heart and heritage – Herefordshire Council
evidence.summary: December 2024 news article confirming completion of public realm improvements in Leominster town centre under the Heritage Action Zone: attractive road surfacing, wider pavements, improved junctions and restored historic shopfronts funded by Historic England, Herefordshire Council and Leominster Town Council.

evidence.title: Business and civic leaders celebrate £60m investment success at Skylon Park – Herefordshire Council
evidence.summary: December 2024 article celebrating £60m of cumulative public-private investment at Skylon Park Enterprise Zone, Hereford, which transformed 110 acres of largely derelict council-owned land into a business park now home to 100+ businesses and 1,500 jobs.

title: Borehamwood Catterick Way – New Social Rent Housing
description: Hertsmere Borough Council began construction in February 2026 on nine new three-bedroom social rent homes on a former derelict garage site on Catterick Way, Borehamwood. This is the first new-build residential scheme delivered entirely by the council itself in many years. Three ground-floor homes include private gardens and are fully wheelchair-adaptable. The development also includes landscaping and secure cycle storage.
statusRationale: Construction officially began in February 2026 with a ground-breaking ceremony; project on site and progressing.
locationDescription: Catterick Way garage site, Borehamwood, Hertsmere

evidence.title: Breaking ground for new social rent homes in Borehamwood – Hertsmere Borough Council
evidence.summary: News article (24 February 2026) confirming construction start on nine three-bedroom social rent homes on Catterick Way. Described as the council's first directly delivered new-build residential scheme in many years, with wheelchair-adaptable units and landscaping.

title: Buxton Spring Gardens – Future High Streets Fund and Heritage Action Zone
description: High Peak Borough Council secured millions of pounds from the Future High Streets Fund (FHSF) and the High Street Heritage Action Zone (HSHAZ) to regenerate Spring Gardens, Buxton's main commercial area. The Council acquired The Springs shopping centre as a milestone in delivering the regeneration. The HSHAZ provided grants for historic shopfront repair: over £350,000 at Ponden Homes and Fitzgerald's Bakery, £300,000 for restoration of the Grade II listed former White Lion pub, and reinstatement of the missing historic urn on the slopes.
statusRationale: FHSF and HSHAZ projects substantially delivered; council ownership of The Springs shopping centre secured; shopfront and heritage grant programme ongoing.
locationDescription: Spring Gardens shopping area, Buxton town centre, High Peak, Derbyshire

evidence.title: Heritage at the heart of Buxton's regeneration – High Peak Borough Council
evidence.summary: Article (28 June 2023) detailing Future High Streets Fund and Heritage Action Zone investment in Buxton Spring Gardens, council acquisition of The Springs shopping centre, over £350,000 shopfront grants, £300,000 White Lion pub restoration and historic urn reinstatement.

evidence.title: Regeneration projects overview – High Peak Borough Council
evidence.summary: Overview of major regeneration projects in the High Peak, including Buxton Heritage Action Zone, Future High Street Fund, Fairfield Common A6 Roundabout planning permission, and Glossop Town Hall restoration.

title: Cranford Country Park Restoration
description: Hillingdon Council, with funding largely from the National Lottery Heritage Fund and contributions from National Highways and the Heritage of London Trust, carried out a major restoration of Cranford Country Park. Works included restoring the historic stables (repairing dry rot, woodworm, re-roofing and double glazing, air-source heat pump heating), constructing a new café on the footprint of the demolished Cranford House with a green roof, restoring cellars with interpretation displays, enlarging and landscaping the car park, widening access from the A312, and creating a new playground.
statusRationale: Construction works nearing completion; stables fully repaired, café built, car park enlarged and driveway reinstated from the A312.
locationDescription: Cranford Country Park, off the A312, Cranford, London Borough of Hillingdon

evidence.title: Cranford Park project – London Borough of Hillingdon
evidence.summary: Council project page describing extensive restoration of Cranford Country Park: stables repair with air-source heat pumps, new café with green roof on the Cranford House footprint, restored cellars with interpretation, enlarged car park, new playground, and widened A312 driveway. Funded mainly by National Lottery Heritage Fund.

title: Twycross Zoo – National Science and Conservation Centre
description: Following a successful bid to the Levelling Up Fund Round 1 in 2021, Hinckley and Bosworth Borough was awarded £19.9 million to deliver a purpose-built National Science and Conservation Centre adjacent to Twycross Zoo. The centre will provide world-class facilities for researchers to study the four great apes and over 100 endangered species, as well as public engagement and education space.
statusRationale: £19.9m LUF Round 1 award confirmed; application form and appendices published; project in delivery.
locationDescription: Twycross Zoo, Norton-juxta-Twycross, Hinckley and Bosworth, Leicestershire

evidence.title: Levelling Up Fund 2021 – successful bid for funding – Hinckley and Bosworth Borough Council
evidence.summary: Council page confirming the £19,900,000 Levelling Up Fund Round 1 award for a National Science and Conservation Centre at Twycross Zoo, providing a purpose-built research facility to study the four great apes and over 100 endangered species.

title: Feltham Town Centre Regeneration
description: The London Borough of Hounslow is delivering a major regeneration programme for Feltham town centre, identified as a District Centre in the London Plan 2021. Works already completed include repaving and widening footways, new street lighting, additional pedestrian crossings, new cycle lanes, lengthened bus lanes, resurfaced carriageways and new street furniture. Feltham Green and Bridge House Pond have received substantial public realm improvements. A Feltham Masterplan 2026 sets out the council's vision for the town's continued transformation, taking advantage of its proximity to Heathrow Airport and Feltham train station's connections.
statusRationale: Extensive public realm and transport improvements already delivered; Feltham Masterplan 2026 adopted to guide next phases of investment.
locationDescription: Feltham town centre, London Borough of Hounslow

evidence.title: Feltham town centre regeneration – London Borough of Hounslow
evidence.summary: Council regeneration page describing completed improvements to pavements, street lighting, crossings, cycle lanes and public spaces in Feltham, plus the Feltham Masterplan 2026 vision for continued regeneration of this district centre near Heathrow.

title: Hinchingbrooke Country Park Redevelopment
description: Huntingdonshire District Council contracted Lindum Group in March 2026 to deliver a transformative redevelopment of the 150-acre Hinchingbrooke Country Park in Huntingdon. Funded through the Community Infrastructure Levy (CIL) and capital budgets, the project will enhance biodiversity through targeted habitat management, redesign the car park with additional spaces, accessible bays and EV charging points, and resurface pathways to improve access for all visitors. Works commence April 2026 with completion expected Spring 2027.
statusRationale: Contractor (Lindum Group) appointed 26 March 2026, construction start confirmed for 13 April 2026, funded through CIL and capital budgets.
locationDescription: Hinchingbrooke Country Park, Huntingdon, Cambridgeshire

evidence.title: Contractor appointed for Hinchingbrooke Country Park redevelopment – Huntingdonshire District Council
evidence.summary: News article (26 March 2026) confirming Lindum Group awarded contract for Hinchingbrooke Country Park. Work starts 13 April 2026, completing Spring 2027. CIL and capital funded; delivers improved car park with EV charging, better accessibility and habitat management across 150 acres.

evidence.title: Works underway on Priory Centre redevelopment in St Neots – Huntingdonshire District Council
evidence.summary: June 2025 article confirming construction start on the Priory Centre refurbishment and expansion in St Neots, part of the Market Towns Programme. £1.95m contribution from St Neots Town Council. Will provide a modern cultural, social and community hub for the market town.

title: Accrington Market Hall and Town Centre Levelling Up Programme
description: Hyndburn Borough Council received a confirmed allocation from the Government's Levelling Up Fund following its August 2022 bid. The programme covers three interventions: (1) refurbishment and transformation of the iconic Victorian Accrington Market Hall into a food-and-drink destination whilst retaining 50% traditional retail space; (2) acquisition and refurbishment of Market Chambers opposite the Market Hall into a cultural, arts and heritage venue; and (3) acquisition and restoration of Burton's Chambers as a co-working space for businesses and events.
statusRationale: LUF allocation confirmed February 2023; detailed plans shared with Market Hall traders. Delivery subject to procurement and planning processes; original March 2025 target has passed.
locationDescription: Accrington Market Hall and town centre, Hyndburn, Lancashire

evidence.title: Accrington Market Hall Levelling Up Plans – Hyndburn Borough Council
evidence.summary: February 2023 news article confirming LUF allocation for Hyndburn, detailing plans for Accrington Market Hall (50% food and drink, 50% traditional retail), Market Chambers (cultural venue) and Burton's Chambers (co-working space). Proposed completion by March 2025.

evidence.title: Levelling Up Fund Bid Submission – Hyndburn Borough Council
evidence.summary: September 2022 article by Council Leader confirming submission of LUF bid covering Accrington Market Hall, Market Chambers and Burton's Chambers, drawn from the Council's Town Centre Investment Plan for Accrington.

title: Ipswich Regent Theatre Redevelopment
description: Ipswich Borough Council approved plans and appointed contractor Mixbrow Construction in February 2025 to deliver a major refurbishment of the Grade II listed Ipswich Regent Theatre on St Helen's Street. The project modernises and improves the 1929 Art Deco theatre: a new illuminated Art Deco canopy, improved accessible entrance with new lift and Wall of Fame mural, restored full-height ceilings and original Art Deco detailing, extended bars and a Changing Places facility. The theatre closed May 2025 and is scheduled to reopen for the Christmas pantomime in December 2025. The project is funded entirely by a Restoration Levy ring-fenced since June 2023.
statusRationale: Contractor appointed February 2025, construction underway from May 2025, grand reopening planned December 2025. Fully funded through ring-fenced Restoration Levy.
locationDescription: Ipswich Regent Theatre, St Helen's Street, Ipswich town centre, Suffolk

evidence.title: Ipswich Regent Redevelopment – Ipswich Borough Council
evidence.summary: Council project page (December 2024) confirming planning approval for the Regent Theatre redevelopment, detailing all improvements including new canopy, accessible entrance, restored Art Deco features, extended bars, Changing Places facility. Funded by ring-fenced Restoration Levy.

evidence.title: Contractor awarded works on Ipswich Regent Theatre redevelopment – Ipswich Borough Council
evidence.summary: News article (26 February 2025) confirming Mixbrow Construction appointed. Theatre closes 4 May 2025 and reopens December 2025 for Cinderella pantomime. Shows hosted at Corn Exchange during closure.

title: Islington Council New Homes on Estates Programme
description: Islington Council is delivering a rolling programme of new council homes on underused estate land, building genuinely affordable housing while improving communal areas, landscaping and facilities. Active sites as of early 2026 include: Andover Estate N7 (42 homes including affordable workspace, completing February 2026), Elthorne Estate N19 (46 homes with new multi-games court at Zoffany Park, completing January 2026), Park View Estate N5 (31 new council homes) and Beaumont Rise N19 (27 homes including 17 supported housing units). Future schemes include Bemerton Estate South (84 homes, planning approved September 2025) and Vorley Road (planned Spring 2027 start).
statusRationale: Multiple sites in active construction with recent completions across north Islington; programme is council-wide and ongoing with new schemes in planning.
locationDescription: Various council estates across the London Borough of Islington including Andover Estate N7, Elthorne Estate N19 and Park View Estate N5

evidence.title: Where we are building – Islington Council
evidence.summary: Council page listing all active new-build housing development sites: Andover Estate (42 homes), Elthorne Estate (46 homes), Park View Estate (31 homes), Beaumont Rise (27 homes including supported housing) and Bemerton Estate South (84 homes, planning approved September 2025).

title: Grenfell Tower Site and Memorial Commission
description: The Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea is managing the long-term recovery, memorialisation and redevelopment of the Grenfell Tower site. The Grenfell Tower Site and Memorial Commission is developing a permanent national memorial following extensive consultation with bereaved families, survivors and the wider community. The Council also continues to fulfil its 45 commitments to the Grenfell Tower Inquiry. A Grenfell Support programme 2024–2028 provides a new four-year programme of support for survivors and the community. The tower wrapped in white remains a site of profound significance in North Kensington.
statusRationale: Memorialisation and site planning ongoing; permanent memorial design process under way. Recovery programme and rehousing largely complete but memorial and site future not yet finalised.
locationDescription: Grenfell Tower site, Lancaster West Estate, North Kensington, Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea

evidence.title: Grenfell response and recovery – Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea
evidence.source: Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea
evidence.summary: Council's main page for Grenfell response, covering the Grenfell Tower Inquiry commitments, the Memorial Commission and site planning, Grenfell Support 2024–2028, rehousing programme, NHS support services and the Independent Grenfell Recovery Taskforce.

title: King's Lynn Pride in Place Programme and St George's Guildhall Creative Hub
description: The Government approved King's Lynn's Pride in Place Regeneration Plan on 25 March 2026, unlocking £20 million of national investment over ten years to revitalise the town centre and strengthen communities. The plan covers repurposing the town centre, enhanced walking and cycling routes, and neighbourhood initiatives for North Lynn, South Lynn, Gaywood Fairstead and the town centre. The flagship St George's Guildhall Creative Hub renovation—a medieval Guildhall being transformed into a national and international arts, theatre and creative industries destination—commenced construction November 2025 and is scheduled to reopen in 2028. Highways safety and public realm improvements on King Street adjacent to the Guildhall were confirmed in March 2026.
statusRationale: Pride in Place Regeneration Plan formally approved by Government 25 March 2026; Guildhall construction underway from November 2025 targeting 2028 reopening; public realm works on King Street confirmed.
locationDescription: King's Lynn town centre, with St George's Guildhall on King Street, King's Lynn, Norfolk

evidence.title: Major boost for King's Lynn as Government approves Pride in Place Regeneration Plan
evidence.source: Borough Council of King's Lynn and West Norfolk
evidence.summary: 25 March 2026 article confirming Government approval of the King's Lynn Pride in Place Regeneration Plan, releasing £20m over ten years. Key projects include repurposing the town centre, enhanced walking and cycling routes, and community initiatives in priority neighbourhoods shaped by 1,100-person consultation.

evidence.title: Highways safety and public realm improvements planned for Guildhall renovation project
evidence.source: Borough Council of King's Lynn and West Norfolk
evidence.summary: 23 March 2026 article announcing public realm and highway improvements outside St George's Guildhall on King Street: widened pavements, bicycle racks, delivery bay, new benches, sculpture, and four new street trees. Main Guildhall works started November 2025 with 2028 reopening target.

title: Hull Orchard Park Regeneration – Dane Park New Council Homes
description: Hull City Council is continuing its Orchard Park regeneration programme with the construction of 99 new council homes for rent at the Dane Park site (former Dane Park Primary School). Construction began April 2024 with phased completions through to 2027. An additional 34 homes are under construction at nearby Isledane from 2025. Homes use a fabric-first approach exceeding insulation standards, incorporate sustainable urban drainage systems (SuDS) designed with Yorkshire Water, and approximately half exceed current UK accessibility standards. Orchard Park is identified in Hull's Local Plan as a housing regeneration priority area.
statusRationale: Construction started April 2024 at Dane Park with phased completions; Isledane site also under construction from 2025. Part of Hull's established Local Plan regeneration priority.
locationDescription: Dane Park site (former Dane Park Primary School), Orchard Park, north Kingston upon Hull

evidence.title: Orchard Park regeneration – Hull City Council
evidence.summary: Council regeneration page confirming 99 new council homes for rent at Dane Park started construction April 2024 (completing in phases to 2027), plus 34 further homes at Isledane from 2025. Features fabric-first energy efficiency, SuDS flood management with Yorkshire Water, and high accessibility standards.

title: New Malden Town Centre Regeneration
description: The Royal Borough of Kingston upon Thames is delivering a series of regeneration projects in New Malden town centre, celebrating the area's unique cultural identity as home to Europe's largest Korean community. Key schemes include: Jubilee Square, a new public space, pocket park and pedestrian linkway into Cocks Crescent; regeneration of the Fountain Roundabout and creation of a new pocket park; redevelopment of the underutilised Cocks Crescent site; and Burlington School expansion. Two Go Cycle sustainable transport routes serve New Malden: the Beeline Way (New Malden to Raynes Park) and a Kingston-to-New Malden active travel corridor under construction.
statusRationale: Multiple projects in active delivery including Jubilee Square, Fountain Roundabout improvements and Go Cycle active travel routes; council committed through capital programme and planning obligations.
locationDescription: New Malden town centre, Royal Borough of Kingston upon Thames, south-west London

evidence.title: Projects in New Malden – Royal Borough of Kingston upon Thames
evidence.source: Royal Borough of Kingston upon Thames
evidence.summary: Council regeneration page describing Jubilee Square pocket park, Fountain Roundabout improvements, Cocks Crescent redevelopment, Burlington School expansion, and the Go Cycle active travel programme with Beeline Way to Raynes Park and Kingston town centre route under construction.

title: Huddersfield Blueprint – Cultural Heart and Town Centre Regeneration
description: Kirklees Council's flagship £250m+ programme to transform Huddersfield town centre, including demolition and redevelopment of the Piazza shopping centre, creation of a new Cultural Heart (library, gallery and creative space), and refurbishment of Queensgate Market with public realm improvements throughout.
statusRationale: Piazza demolition completed 2024 and Cultural Heart planning application progressed, but revised timelines and value-engineering exercises have introduced delays to the wider programme.
locationDescription: Huddersfield town centre, King Street and New Street area

evidence.title: Huddersfield Blueprint – Kirklees Council
evidence.summary: Official Kirklees Council page for the Huddersfield Blueprint regeneration programme, covering the Cultural Heart, Piazza redevelopment and market improvements.

title: Prescot Town Centre Regeneration – Post-Shakespeare North Phase
description: Following the opening of the Shakespeare North Playhouse in 2022, Knowsley Council is leading phase 2 of the Prescot town centre regeneration, including public realm improvements, heritage-led commercial development and new housing around the playhouse, funded by Levelling Up and UK Shared Prosperity Fund allocations.
statusRationale: Council confirmed continued investment; phase 2 public realm and housing works are proceeding within programme with UKSPF and LUHBF support.
locationDescription: Prescot town centre, Knowsley, Merseyside

evidence.title: Regeneration – Knowsley Council
evidence.summary: Knowsley Council regeneration hub covering ongoing investment in Prescot, Kirkby and Huyton town centres including the post-Shakespeare North phase.

title: Brixton Town Centre Masterplan
description: Lambeth Council's Brixton Town Centre Masterplan guides investment in public realm, transport connectivity and housing across the Brixton area. It includes improvements to Brixton Market and covered arcades, Windrush Square enhancements, and key transport interchange upgrades, supported by the Greater London Authority.
statusRationale: Masterplan adopted; public realm phases under delivery with GLA and UKSPF support; Windrush Square works completed in 2024.
locationDescription: Brixton town centre, London Borough of Lambeth

evidence.title: Major Developments and Regeneration – Lambeth Council
evidence.summary: Lambeth Council's major developments and regeneration hub, covering the Brixton Town Centre Masterplan and borough-wide investment schemes.

title: Canal Corridor North Regeneration, Lancaster
description: Lancaster City Council's Canal Corridor North regeneration scheme brings forward mixed-use development on former industrial land north of Lancaster city centre, alongside the Lancaster Canal. The allocated site will deliver housing, employment floorspace and public open space, requiring significant infrastructure investment.
statusRationale: Site allocated in adopted Local Plan; detailed masterplan work and infrastructure funding discussions ongoing; delivery timeline subject to resolution of transport interventions.
locationDescription: Canal Corridor North, Lancaster city centre, Lancashire

evidence.title: Canal Corridor North – Lancaster City Council
evidence.summary: Lancaster City Council planning policy page for the Canal Corridor North strategic regeneration site, covering allocations, masterplan principles and infrastructure requirements.

title: West Yorkshire Mass Transit Programme
description: West Yorkshire Combined Authority (WYCA) is developing a mass transit network for the Leeds City Region, with corridors connecting Leeds, Bradford, Halifax and beyond. The programme is examining tram-train or light rail technology and forms the centrepiece of the region's transport investment pipeline, backed by the Government's City Region Sustainable Transport Settlement.
statusRationale: Outline Business Case progressing; preferred route options announced 2024 with development funding secured, but full funding commitment and statutory processes remain pending.
locationDescription: Leeds city centre and West Yorkshire transport corridors

evidence.title: Mass Transit – West Yorkshire Combined Authority
evidence.summary: WYCA page detailing the West Yorkshire Mass Transit programme, preferred route options, investment scale and programme milestones.

title: Leicester Waterside Regeneration
description: Leicester City Council's Waterside regeneration is transforming a former industrial area between the city centre and the River Soar, delivering over 1,000 new homes, offices, hotels and public spaces. The scheme includes new pedestrian and cycle bridges and extensive public realm along the river corridor.
statusRationale: Multiple phases completed or under construction; new residential buildings and public realm delivering to programme through 2024-25.
locationDescription: Waterside area, west of Leicester city centre, alongside the River Soar

evidence.title: Waterside Regeneration – Leicester City Council
evidence.summary: Leicester City Council's Waterside regeneration page covering masterplan, planning history, completed phases and ongoing development activity.

title: Newhaven Town Deal Capital Programme
description: Newhaven (within Lewes District) secured a £25.6 million Town Deal in 2021, with capital projects including the Newhaven Enterprise Centre, flood and coastal resilience works, Newhaven Fort heritage improvements, and transport and active travel upgrades. Delivery is continuing through to 2026 and is led by Lewes District Council.
statusRationale: Multiple Town Deal projects delivered or under construction; Enterprise Centre opened 2024; coastal defence works progressing on programme.
locationDescription: Newhaven, East Sussex (within Lewes District)

evidence.title: Newhaven Town Deal – Lewes District Council
evidence.summary: Lewes District Council page for the Newhaven Town Deal programme, covering the £25.6m investment, funded projects and delivery milestones.

title: Lewisham Gateway Phase 2
description: Lewisham Gateway is a major mixed-use regeneration scheme at Lewisham town centre, delivering over 600 homes, offices, retail and public realm. Phase 2 involves further residential towers and completion of the riverside public realm adjacent to Lewisham DLR and rail station, led by Lewisham Council in partnership with Muse Developments.
statusRationale: Phase 1 complete and occupied; Phase 2 under construction and delivering to agreed programme.
locationDescription: Lewisham town centre, adjacent to Lewisham DLR and National Rail station

evidence.title: Lewisham Gateway – London Borough of Lewisham
evidence.summary: Lewisham Council project page for the Lewisham Gateway mixed-use development covering housing, commercial and public realm delivery.

title: Lichfield District Leisure Centre Replacement
description: Lichfield District Council is progressing plans to replace the ageing Friary Grange Leisure Centre with a modern community leisure facility. The project includes a new swimming pool, sports halls and fitness facilities serving the district.
statusRationale: Business case approved by council; procurement and detailed design underway but programme subject to cost review in the context of construction inflation.
locationDescription: Lichfield, Staffordshire

evidence.title: Leisure and Sport – Lichfield District Council
evidence.summary: Lichfield District Council page covering leisure investment and the planned replacement community leisure centre.

title: Lincoln Cornhill Quarter Regeneration
description: City of Lincoln Council's Cornhill Quarter programme has transformed the area around Lincoln's historic Cornhill, refurbishing the Victorian market hall, creating new public squares, a hotel and commercial units. Wider public realm improvements and connectivity works are continuing into 2025 under the council's city centre investment programme.
statusRationale: Core Cornhill Quarter works substantially complete; market hall operational; ongoing connectivity and public realm phases continuing to deliver.
locationDescription: Cornhill Quarter, Lincoln city centre, Lincolnshire

evidence.title: Regeneration – City of Lincoln Council
evidence.summary: Lincoln Council regeneration page covering the Cornhill Quarter programme and wider city centre investment.

title: Liverpool Waters – Central Docks and Princes Dock Phases
description: Liverpool Waters is Peel L&P's consented multi-decade regeneration of 60 hectares of northern docklands, backed by Liverpool City Council. The Central Docks and Princes Dock phases are delivering over 1,000 homes, offices, hotels and public waterfront spaces, forming a key component of the Liverpool City Region's growth agenda.
statusRationale: Consistent delivery across multiple phases; residential buildings completing in 2024-25 and commercial occupiers progressing.
locationDescription: Liverpool northern docklands, Princes Dock to Central Docks

evidence.title: Liverpool Waters – Liverpool Vision
evidence.source: Liverpool Vision (Liverpool City Council economic development company)
evidence.summary: Liverpool Vision project page for Liverpool Waters covering Northern Docks regeneration, investment pipeline and current phase delivery status.

title: Luton DART – Direct Air-Rail Transit
description: The Luton DART is the UK's first fully automated personal rapid transit system, connecting Luton Airport Parkway station to London Luton Airport terminal. The system opened in March 2024, delivering a 4-minute shuttle journey and replacing the legacy bus service as part of Luton Rising's wider airport growth strategy.
statusRationale: System opened March 2024 and is in full commercial operation, delivering planned passenger connectivity to the airport.
locationDescription: London Luton Airport, connecting Parkway rail station to airport terminal

evidence.title: DART – Luton Rising
evidence.source: Luton Rising (London Luton Airport Ltd / Luton Borough Council)
evidence.summary: Luton Rising's DART page confirming the March 2024 opening and operational details of the automated people mover.

title: Maidstone Town Centre Future High Streets Investment
description: Maidstone Borough Council secured Future High Streets Fund and UK Shared Prosperity Fund investment to revitalise Maidstone town centre, including public realm improvements at Week Street, enhanced pedestrian links, independent retail support and wayfinding improvements.
statusRationale: Town centre public realm works completed or substantially complete; programme largely delivered within the funding window.
locationDescription: Maidstone town centre, Week Street and Market area, Kent

evidence.title: Regeneration – Maidstone Borough Council
evidence.summary: Maidstone Borough Council regeneration page covering Future High Streets Fund and UKSPF-funded town centre investment.

title: Manchester Northern Gateway
description: Manchester Northern Gateway is one of the UK's largest urban regeneration schemes, covering 155 hectares north of Manchester city centre. Manchester City Council and Manchester City Football Group (MCFG) are collaborating on a 15,000-home development with significant employment land, new schools and major public infrastructure investment in Collyhurst and Miles Platting.
statusRationale: Masterplan approved; early housing and infrastructure delivery underway in Collyhurst; full programme scale requires sustained long-term investment over 15+ years.
locationDescription: North Manchester, Collyhurst and Miles Platting

evidence.title: Northern Gateway – Manchester City Council
evidence.summary: Manchester City Council page for the Northern Gateway regeneration scheme, covering masterplan, housing delivery and infrastructure investment pipeline.

title: Mansfield Town Centre Levelling Up Fund Regeneration
description: Mansfield District Council received Levelling Up Fund investment to regenerate Mansfield town centre, including improvements to the Market Place public realm, the Four Seasons Shopping Centre environs, and transport connectivity enhancements supporting the wider town centre economy.
statusRationale: Levelling Up funding awarded and projects scoped; public realm elements progressing but commercial regeneration elements face viability challenges in the current retail market.
locationDescription: Mansfield town centre, Market Place, Nottinghamshire

evidence.title: Regeneration – Mansfield District Council
evidence.summary: Mansfield District Council regeneration page covering Levelling Up Fund projects and town centre investment programme.

title: Rochester Riverside Mixed-Use Development
description: Rochester Riverside is Medway Council's flagship regeneration project transforming 13 hectares of brownfield land on the south bank of the River Medway into a new mixed-use neighbourhood with over 1,400 homes, a new primary school, a riverside park and commercial space.
statusRationale: Multiple residential phases completed and occupied; primary school open; later phases continuing to deliver to programme into 2025.
locationDescription: Rochester Riverside, south bank of the River Medway, Rochester, Kent

evidence.title: Rochester Riverside – Medway Council
evidence.summary: Medway Council page for Rochester Riverside covering phases, housing delivery, school and riverside park completion.

title: Melton Mowbray Northern Distributor Road
description: A new northern distributor road around Melton Mowbray to relieve chronic congestion on the A607 and A606, unlock strategic housing allocations and improve freight routing. Promoted by Leicestershire County Council with strong support from Melton Borough Council, the scheme is identified as critical infrastructure for the town's growth.
statusRationale: Scheme has faced repeated funding gaps; full construction funding not yet secured as of 2024-25 and the programme has been significantly delayed.
locationDescription: Northern fringe of Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire

evidence.title: Melton Mowbray Northern Distributor Road – Leicestershire County Council
evidence.summary: Leicestershire County Council page for the Melton Mowbray Northern Distributor Road covering route, funding status and programme timeline.

title: Morden Town Centre Regeneration
description: Merton Council is leading the regeneration of Morden town centre, working with developers to deliver new housing, improved public spaces and enhanced connectivity at Morden Underground station, building on the Mayor of London's Housing Zone designation for the area.
statusRationale: Masterplan adopted and some development sites advancing through planning; overall delivery slower than originally projected due to viability challenges.
locationDescription: Morden town centre, adjacent to Northern line terminus, London Borough of Merton

evidence.title: Regeneration – Merton Council

title: Gateway 14 Business Park, Stowmarket

locationDescription: Bideford town centre and quay, Devon

evidence.title: Bideford Masterplan Launch
evidence.summary: Torridge District Council launches the Bideford Regeneration Strategy and Masterplan 2026–2031, setting out investment priorities for the town centre, quay, and local economy.

title: St George's Leisure Centre Redevelopment – £67m
description: Tower Hamlets Council is progressing plans for a £67 million redevelopment of St George's Leisure Centre in Shadwell, combining upgraded sports and leisure facilities with new affordable homes on the site.
statusRationale: Plans progressed and publicly announced March 2026; planning application and procurement stages ahead.
locationDescription: St George's Leisure Centre, Shadwell, Tower Hamlets

evidence.title: Investing in health and homes: Tower Hamlets progresses plans to upgrade leisure facilities and build new homes
evidence.summary: Tower Hamlets Council announces £67m plans to redevelop St George's Leisure Centre in Shadwell, combining upgraded leisure facilities with new affordable homes.

title: Chester House – Proposed 1,200-Home Neighbourhood, Stretford
description: Trafford Council has reached a major milestone in proposals for a new 1,200-home neighbourhood at the former Greater Manchester Police headquarters (Chester House) in Stretford, with plans for homes, community facilities, and public open space on the brownfield site.
statusRationale: Milestone reached March 2026 on planning and design stage; full planning consent and construction not yet confirmed.
locationDescription: Chester House (former GMP HQ), Stretford, Trafford

evidence.title: Milestone reached: proposed new 1,200-home neighbourhood
evidence.summary: Trafford Council announces a milestone in the Chester House regeneration proposals, advancing plans for a 1,200-home neighbourhood on the brownfield site of the former Greater Manchester Police headquarters in Stretford.

title: Science Vale Enterprise Zones
description: Vale of White Horse District Council supports the Science Vale Enterprise Zones around Harwell and Milton Park in Oxfordshire, major nationally-significant research and innovation campuses attracting investment in science, technology, and high-value employment.
statusRationale: Enterprise Zones are established and operational with ongoing inward investment; council maintains a dedicated web presence.
locationDescription: Harwell and Milton Park, Vale of White Horse, Oxfordshire

evidence.title: Science Vale Enterprise Zones
evidence.source: Vale of White Horse District Council
evidence.summary: Vale of White Horse District Council's page on the Science Vale Enterprise Zones at Harwell and Milton Park, major science and technology campuses in Oxfordshire.

title: Wakefield Cathedral Quarter Transformation
description: Wakefield Council is delivering the Cathedral Quarter Transformation programme to regenerate the historic heart of Wakefield city centre, improving public realm, connectivity, and heritage assets around the cathedral precinct.
statusRationale: Dedicated project page on council's regeneration portal confirms active delivery of the Cathedral Quarter programme.
locationDescription: Cathedral Quarter, Wakefield city centre

evidence.title: Cathedral Quarter Transformation
evidence.summary: Wakefield Council's project page for the Cathedral Quarter Transformation, outlining regeneration plans for the historic heart of Wakefield city centre.
